---
title: Experience
---

## Education
**Oregon State University** \| Corvallis, OR \| B.S. Marine Biology, Minor in Statistics \| Expected June, 2026

## Work experience
**R-Ladies Corvallis** | Co-founder | January, 2026 - Present
<!-- ** ** bolds something -->

<ul style="margin-top: -1em;">
  <li>Co-founded and helped organize a Corvallis chapter focused on building community among R users and hosting workshops and events for members.</li>
  <li>Manage meeting coordination, planning, and outreach.</li>
</ul>

**Oregon State University** | Statistics Learning Assistant | September, 2024 - Present

<ul style="margin-top: -1em;">
  <li>Assisted students in grasping and applying statistical concepts for data-driven problem solving.</li>
  <li>Demonstrated techniques and supported assignments using R.</li>
  <li>Collaborated with instructors to improve course materials and enhance student learning experiences.</li>
</ul>
<!-- ul = unordered list (the whole bullet list container), 
     li = list item (each individual bullet point),
     using raw HTML here instead of markdown dashes so I can apply inline CSS styling -->

**Dutch Bros Coffee** | Barista/Shift Lead | May, 2024 - Present

<ul style="margin-top: -1em;">
  <li>Led shift operations; coordinating team tasks, delegating responsibilities, and ensuring smooth workflow.</li>
  <li>Adapted quickly to changing priorities and fast-paced conditions while maintaining strong performance.</li>
  <li>Fostered a positive team environment through communication, collaboration, and support.</li>

## Relevant Coursework
**Statistical Methods I and II (ST 351, ST 352):**
Study design, descriptive statistics, probability and sampling distributions, hypothesis testing, regression, ANOVA, and logistic regression.

**Methods of Data Analysis I and II (ST 411, ST 412):**
Graphical methods, parametric and nonparametric comparisons, ANOVA, simple and multiple linear regression, model diagnostics, and variable selection.

**Data Visualization (ST 437):**
Visual encoding, design principles, and ethical communication. Emphasis on critiquing and creating publication-ready visualizations.

**Mathematical Statistics I and II (ST 421, ST 422):**
Probability theory, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, sampling distributions, Central Limit Theorem, estimation, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing.

**Use and Abuse of Data: Critical Thinking in Science (BDS 211):**
Critical thinking in data analysis — evaluating logical arguments, identifying unstated assumptions, and recognizing deceptive visualizations, misleading comparisons, and spurious reasoning in the context of biological datasets.

**Computer Programming (CS 201):**
Fundamental programming concepts, computational thinking, and algorithmic problem-solving applied to real-world problems.

**Foundations of Biological Data Sciences (BDS 310):**
Scientific computing and data analysis in Python, including data visualization, reproducible research, and applied problem-solving with real-world life sciences datasets.

**Applied Conservation Biology in Gorongosa National Park (Mozambique) (ENSC 399):**
Hands-on fieldwork in ecological data collection, restoration monitoring, and conservation management. Used R to analyze and communicate field data and scientific findings.

**Statistical Consulting Practicum (ST 407):**
Observation of and participation in a statistical consulting practicum, gaining exposure to real-world data problems and client communication.

## Volunteer Experience
**View Acres Elementary** | Classroom Aide | September, 2014 - May, 2020

<ul style="margin-top: -1em;">
  <li>Logged 500+ hours supporting 4th and 5th grade students in math, reading, and writing.</li>
  <li>Worked directly with students during lessons to reinforce concepts and provide individualized support</li>
  <li>Assisted teacher with classroom activities and helped maintain an engaging learning environment</li>
<ul/>
